subject: Sociology
title: Media Representation
description: A short lesson to help you apply Media Representation.

hook.text: For decades, Hollywood cast white actors in every hero role. If you can't see it, can you be it?

buildup.text: Representation is how media portrays social groups: their visibility, roles, and the stories told about them.

discovery.text: Underrepresentation makes groups invisible. Misrepresentation creates stereotypes that shape real-world treatment.

twist.text: Improved representation changes attitudes. Studies show diverse media reduces prejudice in children and adults.

climax.text: Media representation matters because it shapes who society sees as normal, valuable, and worthy of empathy.

punchline.text: What you see on screen shapes what you see in people.

quiz.question: Why does media representation matter sociologically?
options: It shapes perceptions of who is normal, valuable, and worthy
